mechanism_narrative: Stronger 3-month growth in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 362517 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 20-day return spreads versus lower growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is extremely high or rising, labor-market slack signals weak income growth and credit stress, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 9.5% or rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 9.7 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances show positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate combined with contained inflation indicates accommodative real policy that supports valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation below 3%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.2 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate low-inflation trigger does not raise average 20-day S&P 500 returns above baseline in out-of-sample tests, or if interaction terms are statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and cyclical earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 102.2666 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 3-month growth crossings are followed by non-positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Very low housing starts signal weak construction activity, impaired credit transmission, and poor durable-goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 672 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 700 thousand is not associated with lower subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods above 700 thousand in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains while unemployment remains high identify an early recovery phase with operating leverage and limited wage pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 130570 as of t-1mo
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 9.7 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-positive and high-UNRATE interaction has no incremental explanatory power over PAYEMS alone, or if triggered 60-day returns are not positive on average.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in the federal funds rate from a low base signals withdrawal of liquidity and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25pp over 3 months after being below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.2 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if low-base FEDFUNDS tightening triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or have no negative return spread versus non-trigger periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices combined with falling industrial production signals margin compression and stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation > 3% and INDPRO 3-month growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 217.579 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-acceleration and production-decline interaction does not predict lower 5-day S&P 500 returns than either condition alone in out-of-sample tests.
